    "original_text": "The model may know it is fabricating. The supervisor cannot tell. But the model's computation leaves traces. These signals are byproducts of the computation itself: telemetry that the model cannot separately control under standard training. Unlike the model's testimony (the text it chooses to output), telemetry is harder to fake. We introduce epistemic observability: an interface that exposes this telemetry alongside the standard text output. Epistemic observability is distinct from model interpretability (understanding why a model activates a particular circuit) and from explainability (generating post-hoc rationales for outputs). [
```json
  {
    "section": "Conclusion",
    "original_text": "Systems incorporating language model components must decide how much to spend on verification and where to spend it. This paper provides the cost surface for that decision.",
    "suggested_text": "This paper provides a cost surface to guide verification spending for systems incorporating language model components.",
    "words_saved": 10,
    "explanation": "Merges two sentences and replaces a wordy clause ('must decide how much to spend... and where') with a more concise alternative ('to guide verification spending')."
  },
  {
    "section": "Conclusion",
    "original_text": "Epistemic honesty is unverifiable under text-only observation with bounded supervision, which is a structural property of the interface, not a capability gap. Systems that export structured epistemic state escape this class.",
    "suggested_text": "Epistemic honesty is unverifiable under text-only observation with bounded supervision—a structural interface property, not a capability gap—but systems exporting structured epistemic state escape this limitation.",
    "words_saved": 5,
    "explanation": "Merges two related sentences using an em-dash for conciseness. Shortens 'structural property of the interface' to 'structural interface property'."
  },
  {
    "section": "Conclusion",
    "original_text": "Under length-controlled evaluation, no text-channel feature generalizes across architectures; per-token entropy is the only signal that does.",
    "suggested_text": "Under length-controlled evaluation, no text-channel feature generalizes across architectures; only per-token entropy does.",
    "words_saved": 4,
    "explanation": "Replaces the wordy phrase 'is the only signal that' with a more direct and parallel construction ('only... does')."
  },
  {
    "section": "Conclusion",
    "original_text": "The engineering question is not whether to verify, but how much verification to buy and at which tier.",
    "suggested_text": "The engineering question is not whether to verify, but how much and at which tier.",
    "words_saved": 4,
    "explanation": "Removes redundant words ('verification to buy') as the context is already established by the verb 'verify'."
  }
]
```
